name: Ravi Shankar
abstract: Pandit Ravi Shankar (Bengali pronunciation: [ˈrobi ˈʃɔŋkor]; born Robindro Shaunkor Chowdhury, sometimes spelled as Ravindra Shankar Chowdhury; 7 April 1920 – 11 December 2012) was an Indian sitarist and composer. A sitar virtuoso, he became the world's best-known exponent of Indian classical music in the second half of the 20th century,…

about_quotes.speaker: David Friesen
about_quotes.evidence: To Albert Ayler I guess and Coltrane and Miles, and Ravi Shankar. Those were the people I listened to a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Friesen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I had never seen any of those guys perform. ... I never had the chance to go to one of their concerts
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: you’d hear people like Ravi Shankar and you’d hear all of these kind of exotic instruments
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: From Alice who I later played with and Ravi and the rest of the family.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I remember hearing a recording of Ravi Shankar with Louis Hayes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
